summary: Keyword co-occurrence in CLI bundle requires manual inspection
details: Static patterns matched on the co-occurrence of `ping` and `POST` strings in the bundled CLI files (dist/cli.cjs and dist/cli.js). These are weak signals: `ping` and `POST` appearing in a minified or bundled CLI commonly reflect HTTP client functionality, network diagnostic helpers, or telemetry/health-check endpoints rather than exfiltration. There is no traced-code evidence of credential reads, environment-variable scraping, hardcoded attacker C2 endpoints, install-time lifecycle execution, or other concrete installer-harm fingerprints. The package coordinate (`autotel-terminal`) suggests a terminal/CLI tool where outbound HTTP and ping-style operations may be part of the advertised functionality. Manual review is needed to confirm the destinations and triggers of the matched call sites before any block-grade verdict.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
